calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Receipt Of The Tax Refund
Off Calendar Sep-27-2017 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-20-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gary Lieberman for and with Lorraine Larson.

Mr. Lieberman stated that he was told the $3,000.00 tax refund check is in the mail, explained that the Decedent died five years before the sale of the property and that apparently there was a re-assessment done to the tune of $65,000.00 including penalties. Mr. Lieberman also stated that they appealed it and won, that they got back $64,000.00, that they already received $62,000.00 and that the balance is interest. The Court informed Counsel that the issue of attorney's fees is under submission. The Court granted the Second Amended First and Final Account, etc. (subject to review of the order). Status on filing receipt of the tax refund is set for September 27, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jul-05-2017 Off Calendar

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jul-05-2017 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jun-07-2017 Continued To Jul-05-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Administrator and the Attorney to appear on July 5, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration has not been filed. Status on close of estate is also continued to that date.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Apr-12-2017 Continued To Jun-07-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gary Lieberman for Lorraine Larson.
Evyn Shomer for San Francisco Public Guardian (Trustee of the Christina Kimmons Special Needs Trust).

Mr. Lieberman stated that they won the appeal with the Assessor's Office to recover the $60,000.00 that was deducted from escrow, that they are waiting for the refund from the City Assessor's Office and that it could be a couple of months before they receive it. Mr. Lieberman also stated that other funds are in a blocked account at Chase Bank, that they have been dealing with Chase Bank for two months, that they are being told by the Bank that the order is under review with their Legal Department and that they are not allowed to contact the legal Department, and that the Bank is not honoring the Court's order. Ms. Shomer stated that the Daughter is a Beneficiary, that the Special Needs Trust was created by a substituted judgment and is waiting to be funded, and that Christina is a college student and those expenses could be paid from the Trust. Status on close of the Estate is continued to June 7, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court wants either a Petition for Final Distribution or a Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration filed by then.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

Petition For Preliminary Distribution Kimmons
Off Calendar Dec-28-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gary Lieberman for (petitioner) Lorraine Mae Larson. Grant Tomioka specially appearing for (beneficiary) Christina Kimmons.

Mr. Lieberman apologized to the Court for the mis-handling of this case, that his client is severely disabled, that in his 40 years he has never seen anything like this, that the monies disbursed without a court order have been replaced, that the estate is whole except for the $20,000.00 given to Ms. Kimmons prior to the conservatorship and that the funds are now in 2 blocked accounts. Mr. Tomioka stated that the LPS conservatorship was in place prior to the funds being distributed, that they are not sure if the money was given as cash or a check, that Ms. Kimmons has no recollection of receiving it, that it would jeopardize her benefits, that counsel should have known that she was in a facility and should have contacted her conservator, that the loss of $20,000.00 is significant, that it could be used to pay the Public Guardian and would supplement the special needs trust. The Court asked if the issues can be flushed out at the final distribution. Mr. Tomioka stated that Mr. Lieberman should voluntarily give back the funds or that the State Bar should be contacted regarding the issue. The Court stated that it does not intend to award fees now, that it will require an accounting and will not accept a waiver of accounting. Mr. Tomioka stated that he filed a request for special notice but did not receive copies of filed documents and requested that future documents be emailed to him. Mr. Lieberman stated that he thought copies were served on Mr. Tomioka, that neither he nor his client was aware of the conservatorship at the time of disbursement, that they have not seen any documentation of the conservatorship, that counsel himself did not disburse the funds his client did, that he has a copy of the cancelled check which is endorsed by Christina, that they do not believe that she was under a conservatorship at the time of disbursement, that there has been no impropriety and that the money was to help with moving and living expenses. The Court asked counsel why he took $25,500.00 in fees without a court order. Mr. Lieberman explained that it was for fees incurred prior to the probate and that it has all been replaced. The Court stated that it is not inclined to award fees at this time and that it will require an accounting at the time of final distribution. Mr. Tomioka stated that counsel should have had the funds in a blocked account and that he is accountable for them. The Court took the Petition for Preliminary Distribution under submission.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Chan. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar May-21-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gary Lieberman for (Petitioner) Lorraine Larson.

The Court stated that it will waive publishing of the notice of amendment. Mr. Lieberman reported that there have been no objections by the family. The Court granted the petition for Letters of Administration (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Yeung. (Not Reported)
